speaker: TITANIA
text: Come, sit thee down upon this flow’ry bed,

speaker: TITANIA
text: While I thy amiable cheeks do coy,

speaker: TITANIA
text: And stick muskroses in thy sleek smooth head,

speaker: TITANIA
text: And kiss thy fair large ears, my gentle joy.

speaker: BOTTOM
text: Where’s Peaseblossom?

speaker: PEASEBLOSSOM
text: Ready.

speaker: BOTTOM
text: Scratch my head, Peaseblossom. Where’s

speaker: BOTTOM
text: Monsieur Cobweb?

speaker: COBWEB
text: Ready.

speaker: BOTTOM
text: Monsieur Cobweb, good monsieur, get you

speaker: BOTTOM
text: your weapons in your hand and kill me a red-hipped

speaker: BOTTOM
text: humble-bee on the top of a thistle, and, good

speaker: BOTTOM
text: monsieur, bring me the honey-bag. Do not fret

speaker: BOTTOM
text: yourself too much in the action, monsieur, and,

speaker: BOTTOM
text: good monsieur, have a care the honey-bag break

speaker: BOTTOM
text: not; I would be loath to have you overflown with a

speaker: BOTTOM
text:
honey-bag, signior.
                 
                Cobweb
                 
                exits
                .
               Where’s Monsieur

speaker: BOTTOM
text: Mustardseed?

speaker: MUSTARDSEED
text: Ready.

speaker: BOTTOM
text: Give me your neaf, Monsieur Mustardseed.

speaker: BOTTOM
text: Pray you, leave your courtesy, good monsieur.

speaker: MUSTARDSEED
text: What’s your will?

speaker: BOTTOM
text: Nothing, good monsieur, but to help Cavalery

speaker: BOTTOM
text: Cobweb to scratch. I must to the barber’s,

speaker: BOTTOM
text: monsieur, for methinks I am marvels hairy about

speaker: BOTTOM
text: the face. And I am such a tender ass, if my hair do

speaker: BOTTOM
text: but tickle me, I must scratch.

speaker: TITANIA
text: What, wilt thou hear some music, my sweet love?

speaker: BOTTOM
text: I have a reasonable good ear in music. Let’s

speaker: BOTTOM
text: have the tongs and the bones.

speaker: TITANIA
text: Or say, sweet love, what thou desirest to eat.

speaker: BOTTOM
text: Truly, a peck of provender. I could munch

speaker: BOTTOM
text: your good dry oats. Methinks I have a great desire

speaker: BOTTOM
text: to a bottle of hay. Good hay, sweet hay, hath no

speaker: BOTTOM
text: fellow.

speaker: TITANIA
text: I have a venturous fairy that shall seek

speaker: TITANIA
text: The squirrel’s hoard and fetch thee new nuts.

speaker: BOTTOM
text: I had rather have a handful or two of dried

speaker: BOTTOM
text: peas. But, I pray you, let none of your people stir

speaker: BOTTOM
text: me; I have an exposition of sleep come upon me.

speaker: TITANIA
text: Sleep thou, and I will wind thee in my arms.—

speaker: TITANIA
text: Fairies, begone, and be all ways away.

speaker: TITANIA
text: So doth the woodbine the sweet honeysuckle

speaker: TITANIA
text: Gently entwist; the female ivy so

speaker: TITANIA
text: Enrings the barky fingers of the elm.

speaker: TITANIA
text: O, how I love thee! How I dote on thee!

speaker: OBERON
text: Welcome, good Robin. Seest thou this sweet sight?

speaker: OBERON
text: Her dotage now I do begin to pity.

speaker: OBERON
text: For, meeting her of late behind the wood,

speaker: OBERON
text: Seeking sweet favors for this hateful fool,

speaker: OBERON
text: I did upbraid her and fall out with her.

speaker: OBERON
text: For she his hairy temples then had rounded

speaker: OBERON
text: With coronet of fresh and fragrant flowers;

speaker: OBERON
text: And that same dew, which sometime on the buds

speaker: OBERON
text: Was wont to swell like round and orient pearls,

speaker: OBERON
text: Stood now within the pretty flouriets’ eyes,

speaker: OBERON
text: Like tears that did their own disgrace bewail.

speaker: OBERON
text: When I had at my pleasure taunted her,

speaker: OBERON
text: And she in mild terms begged my patience,

speaker: OBERON
text: I then did ask of her her changeling child,

speaker: OBERON
text: Which straight she gave me, and her fairy sent

speaker: OBERON
text: To bear him to my bower in Fairyland.

speaker: OBERON
text: And now I have the boy, I will undo

speaker: OBERON
text: This hateful imperfection of her eyes.

speaker: OBERON
text: And, gentle Puck, take this transformèd scalp

speaker: OBERON
text: From off the head of this Athenian swain,

speaker: OBERON
text: That he, awaking when the other do,

speaker: OBERON
text: May all to Athens back again repair

speaker: OBERON
text: And think no more of this night’s accidents

speaker: OBERON
text: But as the fierce vexation of a dream.

speaker: OBERON
text: But first I will release the Fairy Queen.

speaker: OBERON
text: Be as thou wast wont to be.

speaker: OBERON
text: See as thou wast wont to see.

speaker: OBERON
text: Dian’s bud o’er Cupid’s flower

speaker: OBERON
text: Hath such force and blessèd power.

speaker: OBERON
text: Now, my Titania, wake you, my sweet queen.

speaker: TITANIA
text: My Oberon, what visions have I seen!

speaker: TITANIA
text: Methought I was enamored of an ass.

speaker: OBERON
text: There lies your love.

speaker: TITANIA
text: How came these things to pass?

speaker: TITANIA
text: O, how mine eyes do loathe his visage now!

speaker: OBERON
text: Silence awhile.—Robin, take off this head.—

speaker: OBERON
text: Titania, music call; and strike more dead

speaker: OBERON
text: Than common sleep of all these five the sense.

speaker: TITANIA
text: Music, ho, music such as charmeth sleep!

speaker: ROBIN
text: Now, when thou wak’st, with thine own fool’s eyes peep.

speaker: OBERON
text: Sound music.

speaker: OBERON
text: Come, my queen, take hands with me,

speaker: OBERON
text: And rock the ground whereon these sleepers be.

speaker: OBERON
text: Now thou and I are new in amity,

speaker: OBERON
text: And will tomorrow midnight solemnly

speaker: OBERON
text: Dance in Duke Theseus’ house triumphantly,

speaker: OBERON
text: And bless it to all fair prosperity.

speaker: OBERON
text: There shall the pairs of faithful lovers be

speaker: OBERON
text: Wedded, with Theseus, all in jollity.

speaker: ROBIN
text: Fairy king, attend and mark.

speaker: ROBIN
text: I do hear the morning lark.

speaker: OBERON
text: Then, my queen, in silence sad

speaker: OBERON
text: Trip we after night’s shade.

speaker: OBERON
text: We the globe can compass soon,

speaker: OBERON
text: Swifter than the wand’ring moon.

speaker: TITANIA
text: Come, my lord, and in our flight

speaker: TITANIA
text: Tell me how it came this night

speaker: TITANIA
text: That I sleeping here was found

speaker: TITANIA
text: With these mortals on the ground.

speaker: THESEUS
text: Go, one of you, find out the Forester.

speaker: THESEUS
text: For now our observation is performed,

speaker: THESEUS
text: And, since we have the vaward of the day,

speaker: THESEUS
text: My love shall hear the music of my hounds.

speaker: THESEUS
text: Uncouple in the western valley; let them go.

speaker: THESEUS
text: Dispatch, I say, and find the Forester.

speaker: THESEUS
text: We will, fair queen, up to the mountain’s top

speaker: THESEUS
text: And mark the musical confusion

speaker: THESEUS
text: Of hounds and echo in conjunction.

speaker: HIPPOLYTA
text: I was with Hercules and Cadmus once,

speaker: HIPPOLYTA
text: When in a wood of Crete they bayed the bear

speaker: HIPPOLYTA
text: With hounds of Sparta. Never did I hear

speaker: HIPPOLYTA
text: Such gallant chiding, for, besides the groves,

speaker: HIPPOLYTA
text: The skies, the fountains, every region near

speaker: HIPPOLYTA
text: Seemed all one mutual cry. I never heard

speaker: HIPPOLYTA
text: So musical a discord, such sweet thunder.

speaker: THESEUS
text: My hounds are bred out of the Spartan kind,

speaker: THESEUS
text: So flewed, so sanded; and their heads are hung

speaker: THESEUS
text: With ears that sweep away the morning dew;

speaker: THESEUS
text: Crook-kneed, and dewlapped like Thessalian bulls;

speaker: THESEUS
text: Slow in pursuit, but matched in mouth like bells,

speaker: THESEUS
text: Each under each. A cry more tunable

speaker: THESEUS
text: Was never holloed to, nor cheered with horn,

speaker: THESEUS
text: In Crete, in Sparta, nor in Thessaly.

speaker: THESEUS
text: Judge when you hear.—But soft! What nymphs are these?

speaker: EGEUS
text: My lord, this is my daughter here asleep,

speaker: EGEUS
text: And this Lysander; this Demetrius is,

speaker: EGEUS
text: This Helena, old Nedar’s Helena.

speaker: EGEUS
text: I wonder of their being here together.

speaker: THESEUS
text: No doubt they rose up early to observe

speaker: THESEUS
text: The rite of May, and hearing our intent,

speaker: THESEUS
text: Came here in grace of our solemnity.

speaker: THESEUS
text: But speak, Egeus. Is not this the day

speaker: THESEUS
text: That Hermia should give answer of her choice?

speaker: EGEUS
text: It is, my lord.

speaker: THESEUS
text: Go, bid the huntsmen wake them with their horns.

speaker: THESEUS
text: Good morrow, friends. Saint Valentine is past.

speaker: THESEUS
text: Begin these woodbirds but to couple now?

speaker: LYSANDER
text: Pardon, my lord.

speaker: THESEUS
text: I pray you all, stand up.

speaker: THESEUS
text: I know you two are rival enemies.

speaker: THESEUS
text: How comes this gentle concord in the world,

speaker: THESEUS
text: That hatred is so far from jealousy

speaker: THESEUS
text: To sleep by hate and fear no enmity?

speaker: LYSANDER
text: My lord, I shall reply amazèdly,

speaker: LYSANDER
text: Half sleep, half waking. But as yet, I swear,

speaker: LYSANDER
text: I cannot truly say how I came here.

speaker: LYSANDER
text: But, as I think—for truly would I speak,

speaker: LYSANDER
text: And now I do bethink me, so it is:

speaker: LYSANDER
text: I came with Hermia hither. Our intent

speaker: LYSANDER
text: Was to be gone from Athens, where we might,

speaker: LYSANDER
text: Without the peril of the Athenian law—

speaker: EGEUS
text: Enough, enough!—My lord, you have enough.

speaker: EGEUS
text: I beg the law, the law upon his head.

speaker: EGEUS
text: They would have stol’n away.—They would, Demetrius,

speaker: EGEUS
text: Thereby to have defeated you and me:

speaker: EGEUS
text: You of your wife and me of my consent,

speaker: EGEUS
text: Of my consent that she should be your wife.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: My lord, fair Helen told me of their stealth,

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Of this their purpose hither to this wood,

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: And I in fury hither followed them,

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Fair Helena in fancy following me.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: But, my good lord, I wot not by what power

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: (But by some power it is) my love to Hermia,

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Melted as the snow, seems to me now

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: As the remembrance of an idle gaud

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Which in my childhood I did dote upon,

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: And all the faith, the virtue of my heart,

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: The object and the pleasure of mine eye,

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Is only Helena. To her, my lord,

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Was I betrothed ere I saw Hermia.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: But like a sickness did I loathe this food.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: But, as in health, come to my natural taste,

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Now I do wish it, love it, long for it,

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: And will forevermore be true to it.

speaker: THESEUS
text: Fair lovers, you are fortunately met.

speaker: THESEUS
text: Of this discourse we more will hear anon.—

speaker: THESEUS
text: Egeus, I will overbear your will,

speaker: THESEUS
text: For in the temple by and by, with us,

speaker: THESEUS
text: These couples shall eternally be knit.—

speaker: THESEUS
text: And, for the morning now is something worn,

speaker: THESEUS
text: Our purposed hunting shall be set aside.

speaker: THESEUS
text: Away with us to Athens. Three and three,

speaker: THESEUS
text: We’ll hold a feast in great solemnity.

speaker: THESEUS
text: Come, Hippolyta.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: These things seem small and undistinguishable,

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Like far-off mountains turnèd into clouds.

speaker: HERMIA
text: Methinks I see these things with parted eye,

speaker: HERMIA
text: When everything seems double.

speaker: HELENA
text: So methinks.

speaker: HELENA
text: And I have found Demetrius like a jewel,

speaker: HELENA
text: Mine own and not mine own.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Are you sure

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: That we are awake? It seems to me

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: That yet we sleep, we dream. Do not you think

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: The Duke was here and bid us follow him?

speaker: HERMIA
text: Yea, and my father.

speaker: HELENA
text: And Hippolyta.

speaker: LYSANDER
text: And he did bid us follow to the temple.

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: Why, then, we are awake. Let’s follow him,

speaker: DEMETRIUS
text: And by the way let us recount our dreams.

speaker: BOTTOM
text: When my cue comes, call me,

speaker: BOTTOM
text: and I will answer. My next is “Most fair Pyramus.”

speaker: BOTTOM
text: Hey-ho! Peter Quince! Flute the bellows-mender!

speaker: BOTTOM
text: Snout the tinker! Starveling! God’s my life! Stolen

speaker: BOTTOM
text: hence and left me asleep! I have had a most rare

speaker: BOTTOM
text: vision. I have had a dream past the wit of man to say

speaker: BOTTOM
text: what dream it was. Man is but an ass if he go about

speaker: BOTTOM
text: to expound this dream. Methought I was—there

speaker: BOTTOM
text: is no man can tell what. Methought I was and

speaker: BOTTOM
text: methought I had—but man is but a patched fool if

speaker: BOTTOM
text: he will offer to say what methought I had. The eye of

speaker: BOTTOM
text: man hath not heard, the ear of man hath not seen,

speaker: BOTTOM
text: man’s hand is not able to taste, his tongue to

speaker: BOTTOM
text: conceive, nor his heart to report what my dream

speaker: BOTTOM
text: was. I will get Peter Quince to write a ballad of this

speaker: BOTTOM
text: dream. It shall be called “Bottom’s Dream” because

speaker: BOTTOM
text: it hath no bottom; and I will sing it in the

speaker: BOTTOM
text: latter end of a play, before the Duke. Peradventure,

speaker: BOTTOM
text: to make it the more gracious, I shall sing it at her

speaker: BOTTOM
text: death.
